content.problemStatement: Write a program using a for loop to calculate the sum of the first 10 natural numbers.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer — the sum of the first 10 natural numbers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program adds the numbers 1 through 10: 1+2+...+10 = 55, which is printed.
Even without any input the program always computes the fixed sum 55.
The result is independent of external data; the sum of the first ten natural numbers is always 55.

solution.approach: The task is to compute 1+2+...+10. This can be done either by a simple for‑loop that adds the numbers from 1 to 10, or by using the arithmetic series formula n*(n+1)/2 with n=10. Both approaches run in constant time because the number of iterations is fixed (10). The program loops over T test cases (T is fixed to 1 because there is no external input) and prints the result for each case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – the loop runs a constant 10 iterations regardless of any input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
